notes: Layer-0 primitives are structural; Layer-1 composites are portable bundles derived from Layer-0.
Comparator (P10) includes two structural flavors: operational (revisable under feedback) and constitutional (guarded under governance).

statements.direct: Systems are separated by boundaries; interaction occurs only through interfaces with conditions.
statements.mirror: What you fail to model as a boundary becomes an invisible coupling.
statements.shadow: Ambiguous boundaries produce leakage, misattribution, and unintended propagation.
statements.shadow_mirror: Over-rigid boundaries produce brittleness and block necessary exchange.

statements.direct: Claims about choice, responsibility, or consent presuppose an agent with sufficient capacity to understand and act.
statements.mirror: When capacity is absent, responsibility shifts to guardianship or protocol.
statements.shadow: Treating low-capacity agents as fully capable produces coercion and false accountability.
statements.shadow_mirror: Over-removing agency suppresses participation and growth.

statements.direct: Cross-boundary intervention requires authorization (consent, mandate, or delegated authority).
statements.mirror: Absent explicit authorization, systems default to force, drift, or accidental violation.
statements.shadow: Unconsented intervention produces breach 🜬 and systemic instability.
statements.shadow_mirror: Excessive gating can paralyze action; delegation mechanisms become necessary.

layer0_primitives.title: Legibility and Interpretability

statements.direct: Authorization and governance require legibility of relevant consequences to affected agents.
statements.mirror: If a decision cannot be explained in the receiver’s frame, it is not truly shared.
statements.shadow: Opaque systems manufacture agreement without understanding.
statements.shadow_mirror: Over-explanation overloads cognition; legibility must be right-sized.

diagnostics: Can the affected party restate the implications?

statements.direct: Flows (energy, time, money, risk, information, responsibility) must be conserved or accounted for across boundaries.
statements.mirror: Unaccounted flows reappear as hidden debt.
statements.shadow: Hidden debt accumulates until forced reconciliation or collapse 🜲.
statements.shadow_mirror: Over-accounting stalls movement; choose appropriate ledger granularity.

layer0_primitives.title: Feedback and Recursion

statements.direct: Actions propagate through feedback loops; systems stabilize, learn, or destabilize through recursion.
statements.mirror: If you do not observe feedback, it will govern you invisibly.
statements.shadow: Unchecked positive feedback amplifies error; delayed feedback causes oscillation.

diagnostics: What are reinforcing vs balancing loops?

statements.direct: Systems drift toward locally rewarded equilibria unless constrained.
statements.mirror: What is rewarded—explicitly or implicitly—becomes dominant behavior.
statements.shadow: Optimization toward proxies (Goodhart drift) detaches behavior from intended purpose.
statements.shadow_mirror: Over-constraint freezes evolution and suppresses exploration.

statements.direct: High-impact actions require reversibility paths proportional to potential harm.
statements.mirror: If reversal is impossible, governance must scale accordingly.
statements.shadow: Irreversible commitments create hostage dynamics and brittle lock-in.
statements.shadow_mirror: Excessive reversibility prevents commitment and coherence.

statements.direct: Controls and governance must scale with system impact (power, reach, irreversibility, externalities).
statements.mirror: Large blast radius demands formal constraint.
statements.shadow: Under-governed power produces abuse and catastrophic risk.
statements.shadow_mirror: Over-governance of low-impact areas wastes capacity and kills agility.

layer0_primitives.title: Distinction and Comparator

statements.direct: Meaning, evaluation, and optimization require distinction; distinction requires a comparator.
statements.mirror: If comparators are not chosen explicitly, they are chosen implicitly by incentives, defaults, or power.
statements.shadow: Hidden or frozen comparators create invisible value systems; optimization then produces “success” that may be reality-misaligned.
statements.shadow_mirror: Over-fluid comparators dissolve commitment and allow manipulation of evaluation criteria.

operational.definition: Comparators used for steering and measurement: metrics, KPIs, thresholds, performance indicators, risk tolerances.
operational.structural_requirement: Operational comparators must be revisable under feedback (P6).
operational.revision_prompt: What is the review cadence, evidence threshold, and authority to revise this comparator?

constitutional.definition: Comparators used for non-tradeable constraints: dignity floors, harm ceilings, rights thresholds, red lines.
constitutional.structural_requirement: Constitutional comparators are revisable only via power-proportionate governance (P9) with explicit legitimacy.
constitutional.revision_prompt: What governance threshold and legitimacy process would be required to revise this comparator?

diagnostics: Who can revise it, how, and how often?

layer1_composites.title: Gradient Generates Flow

statements.direct: Differences across boundaries create gradients; gradients drive flows; flows generate patterns.
statements.mirror: Persistent patterns imply maintained gradients.
statements.shadow: Unmapped gradients create “mysterious” flows that appear irrational until the boundary and differential are exposed.

use_cases: Diagnosing polarization, churn, leakage, talent flight, regulatory arbitrage

layer1_composites.title: Ledgered Reciprocity

statements.direct: Sustained exchange requires mutually reconcilable accounting.
statements.mirror: Every repeated interaction forms a ledger, whether tracked or not.
statements.shadow: Unreconciled exchange accumulates hidden debt that eventually forces correction.

use_cases: Contracts, partnerships, APIs, stakeholder relationships, compensation structures

layer1_composites.title: Compression Distortion

statements.direct: All representations compress reality; optimizing the compression reshapes the system.
statements.mirror: Metrics are maps; confusing the map for the territory produces distortion.
statements.shadow: Proxy optimization detaches systems from lived impact.

use_cases: Dashboards, AI evals, KPIs, performance reviews, regulatory metrics

layer1_composites.title: Granularity Collapse

statements.direct: Under stress, evaluative granularity collapses into coarse binaries.
statements.mirror: Time pressure reduces nuance in comparison.
statements.shadow: Binary framing escalates conflict and removes repair pathways.

use_cases: Crisis governance, polarization, litigation, media narratives

layer1_composites.title: Externalized Harm

statements.direct: When impact crosses boundaries without accounting alignment, harm is externalized.
statements.mirror: If the ledger does not include the affected party, the harm still exists.
statements.shadow: Systems appear successful while exporting suffering outside their accounting boundary.

use_cases: Supply chains, platform governance, finance, AI deployment

statements.direct: Consent varies with capacity, legibility, reversibility, and power balance.
statements.mirror: Binary consent models obscure structural coercion.
statements.shadow: Paper consent without structural support becomes compliance theater.

statements.direct: In hierarchical systems, the operative comparator shifts from “right” to “compliant.”
statements.mirror: Punishment gradients redefine success as obedience.
statements.shadow: Systems scale harm when compliance overrides ethical evaluation.

layer1_composites.title: Causal Attribution Failure

statements.direct: In recursive systems, outcomes are often misattributed to individuals rather than structural loops.
statements.mirror: Scapegoating substitutes narrative simplicity for systemic correction.
statements.shadow: Personal blame prevents loop repair and perpetuates instability.

use_cases: Crisis review, regulatory investigations, organizational failure analysis

layer1_composites.title: Dynamic Stability vs Snapshot Balance

statements.direct: Stability is dynamic correction over time, not static symmetry at a moment.
statements.mirror: Snapshot “balance” can conceal accumulating instability.
statements.shadow: Forcing static balance suppresses sensing and drives instability underground.

use_cases: Public policy, negotiation, staffing models, budget allocation

layer1_composites.title: Level Mismatch (Suboptimization)

statements.direct: Optimizing a subsystem comparator can degrade system-level outcomes when boundaries and feedback are mis-modeled.
statements.mirror: Local success can be global failure when comparators differ across levels.
statements.shadow: Delayed systemic costs accumulate until crisis reveals misalignment.

use_cases: Benchmark vs deployment, quarterly earnings vs tail risk, throughput vs quality

layer1_composites.title: Illegitimate Constitutional Comparator (Sacralized Metric)

statements.direct: An operational comparator becomes de facto constitutional without legitimate governance or explicit ratification.
statements.mirror: When a metric becomes sacred, questioning it becomes taboo.
statements.shadow: Systems reorganize around protecting the metric rather than protecting reality.

use_cases: Institutional critique, governance design, regulatory reform, AI optimization contexts
